The patient is sitting in bed and experiences multiple seizures characterized by a blank stare, head turning to the right, right upper extremity extension or flexion at the elbow (held against the body), and left upper extremity flexion or extension. During some episodes, the patient exhibits facial grimace, head tilted back, mouth open, grunting and groaning sounds, and shaking of the head and body side to side. At times, the patient is lying in bed with eyes closed and not responsive. Throughout the observation, the patient repeatedly shows lack of responsiveness, absence of movement, and sustained posturing during and after seizure activity. Seizure events occur while seated in bed, often with eyes closed, and include repeated motor manifestations consistent with focal to bilateral tonic-clonic activity.
